crisis_name: Student-worker uprising and transfer from Tsiranana to Ramanantsoa
bargain_name: Transfer of full powers from Philibert Tsiranana to General Gabriel Ramanantsoa
document_title: Transfer of full powers from Philibert Tsiranana to General Gabriel Ramanantsoa
condition_summary: elections_referendum_or_roadmap
settlement_effect: Tsiranana handed full powers to General Ramanantsoa to form a government and resolve the 1972 crisis. This was not a negotiated multiparty accord but functioned as the decisive executive settlement.
formalization_sequence: 1972-05-18; Transfer of full powers from Philibert Tsiranana to General Gabriel Ramanantsoa; Tsiranana handed full powers to General Ramanantsoa to form a government and resolve the 1972 crisis. This was not a negotiated multiparty accord but functioned as the decisive executive settlement.; referendum_and_provisional_constitutional_law; E-1972-01; provisional_constitution; SRC_IMODEV_1972
constitutional_context: E-1972-01; substitutes for 1959 Constitution and legalizes military-led provisional government; formalized_transition_or_exception
discourse_evidence: Student-worker mobilization framed the crisis as a rejection of the established order and postcolonial dependence.; The provisional constitutional settlement framed military-led government as necessary to restore order and organize a new constitutional path.
source_ids: SRC_BARG_1972_01; SRC_MARCUS_2004; SRC_IMODEV_1972; DISCSRC_1972_TV5; DISCSRC_1972_MJP_1975; ELTSRC_HOS; ELTSRC_PMVP

bargain_name: 1972 referendum and Loi constitutionnelle provisoire

condition_summary: elections_referendum_or_roadmap

crisis_name: Forces Vives mobilization and 31 October 1991 transition convention
bargain_name: Convention du 31 octobre 1991 / Convention du Panorama
document_title: Convention du 31 octobre 1991 / Convention du Panorama
condition_summary: elections_referendum_or_roadmap
settlement_effect: Ended the 1991 crisis by creating a transitional architecture in which Ratsiraka remained President symbolically while Albert Zafy headed the Haute Autorité de l'État.
formalization_sequence: 1991-10-31; Convention du 31 octobre 1991 / Convention du Panorama; Ended the 1991 crisis by creating a transitional architecture in which Ratsiraka remained President symbolically while Albert Zafy headed the Haute Autorité de l'État.; signed_or_mediated_agreement; E-1991-01; A-1991-01; E-1992-01; C-1992-01; transition_convention; constitutional_amendment; transition_ordinance; constitution; SRC_HCC_MANUEL; SRC_PERSEE_1991; SRC_ICL_1992
constitutional_context: E-1991-01; A-1991-01; E-1992-01; C-1992-01; reallocates authority during democratic transition while maintaining HCC; formalizes 1991 negotiated convention inside constitutional order; implements HCC arrangements under 1991 convention; creates Third Republic semi-presidential constitutional order; formalized_transition_or_exception; formal_or_regular_revision; formal_regular
discourse_evidence: Forces Vives mobilization framed legitimacy through democracy, free elections, and popular pressure against Ratsiraka’s regime.; The 1991 Convention transformed crisis claims into a negotiated transition, combining legality, consensus, and national reconciliation.; The transition convention and later constitutional integration used legal formalization to stabilize an extra-ordinary power-sharing arrangement.; The 1992 Constitution framed the exit from crisis as a restored constitutional order based on democratic institutions and popular sovereignty.; consensus transition to end crisis

bargain_name: Dakar I Agreement
document_title: Dakar I Agreement
condition_summary: claim_resolution; Resolve competing presidential claims through mediated electoral/legal mechanism.; elections_referendum_or_roadmap
settlement_effect: Provided for review/recount of presidential results, referendum if needed, government of national unity, consensus prime minister, and lifting of blockades/violence.
formalization_sequence: 2002-04-18; Dakar I Agreement; Provided for review/recount of presidential results, referendum if needed, government of national unity, consensus prime minister, and lifting of blockades/violence.; signed_or_mediated_agreement
discourse_evidence: Ravalomanana’s claim is coded as electoral mandate discourse: first-round victory, recount, and popular/electoral legitimacy.; Ratsiraka’s counter-claim is coded as constitutional/procedural legality discourse around the disputed election and runoff process.; Dakar/OAU mediation framed the crisis as requiring negotiated verification, stability, and avoidance of civil conflict.; prevent dual-power escalation and stabilize electoral dispute

crisis_name: Ravalomanana–Rajoelina crisis and military-backed transfer to HAT
bargain_name: March 2009 military transfer ordinances and HAT institutional arrangement
document_title: March 2009 military transfer ordinances and HAT institutional arrangement
condition_summary: elections_referendum_or_roadmap
settlement_effect: Power passed from the president to military authority and then to Rajoelina/HAT. This was not a political agreement, but is an equivalent local settlement for executive formation.
formalization_sequence: 2009-03-17; March 2009 military transfer ordinances and HAT institutional arrangement; Power passed from the president to military authority and then to Rajoelina/HAT. This was not a political agreement, but is an equivalent local settlement for executive formation.; ordinance_or_executive_transfer_act; A-2009-01; E-2009-01; E-2009-02; E-2009-03; constitutional_amendment; crisis_transfer_ordinance; transitional_basic_law; SRC_HCC_2009_AMEND; SRC_HCC_2009_ORDS; SRC_ACE_2009_003
constitutional_context: A-2009-01; E-2009-01; E-2009-02; E-2009-03; modifies Article 15 shortly before executive rupture; dissolves government and transfers full powers to Military Directory; transfers full powers from Military Directory to Andry Rajoelina; creates HAT, suspends Senate and National Assembly, defines transition executive powers; formal_or_regular_revision; formalized_transition_or_exception
discourse_evidence: Rajoelina’s 2009 claim is coded as popular/revolutionary legitimacy against alleged authoritarianism and exclusion by Ravalomanana.; Ravalomanana and his supporters framed the 2009 transfer as a coup and defended constitutional continuity/electoral legality.; The one-day Military Directory is coded as an emergency/necessity transfer actor that converted coercive authority into transitional authority.; Ordinance n°2009-003 framed HAT authority through legal organization of public powers after the rupture.

condition_summary: executive_recognition; Recognize vacancy/incapacity and designate authority to exercise head-of-state functions.
settlement_effect: HCC ruled on a request concerning exit from the political and institutional crisis, recognized abandonment/vacancy logic and allowed military authority under Colonel Michaël Randrianirina to exercise head-of-state functions.
